summary: Install-time beacon to hardcoded private IP confirms code execution on installer hosts
details: On `npm install`, this package's preinstall hook (`node index.js` declared in package.json) issues an HTTP GET to the hardcoded private IP `10.107.121.85:8000` at path `/ping_839201`. The path uniquely encodes the package name, the package's own description is `Probe`, the author field is empty, and there is no documented purpose for the network call. This is a dependency-confusion / installer-reconnaissance beacon: the operator of the listening endpoint receives confirmation that the package executed inside a target environment along with the originating network identity (source IP, User-Agent). Even when the destination is an RFC1918 address, publishing a package whose only behavior is to call back to an attacker-chosen host on every install is a supply-chain attack aimed at probing internal networks for code-execution reach.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
